page.title: Marbella Easter Week Yacht Availability & Booking Window

data.meta_description: Check marbella easter week yacht availability for 2025. Book from €749/2h with skipper, drinks & VAT. Secure your spot now.
data.summary: Discover marbella easter week yacht availability for spring 2025. Book early from €749/2h with all inclusions; peak demand means limited slots.
key_facts: Easter Week (Semana Santa) is peak season; book 4-6 weeks ahead.
Fleet departs Puerto Banús; 5-12 pax capacity.
Tier A boats from €749/2h or €2,299/8h; Tier B from €4,719/4h.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, VAT.
Mangusta 80 variants include free jet ski for 4h+ bookings.
Average sea temp in April: 16°C; air temp 18-22°C.
Typical Easter weather: sunny intervals, light wind, low swell.
Last-minute availability rare; WhatsApp for real-time slots.

faq.q: When should I book for Easter Week in Marbella?
faq.a: Book 4 to 6 weeks before Easter Week (Semana Santa) to secure your preferred yacht. March–April is a transitional period with increasing demand, so early booking avoids disappointment, especially for larger groups needing 12-pax vessels.

faq.q: What is the weather like for Easter Week yacht charters?
faq.a: Easter Week in Marbella typically sees daytime highs of 18–22°C and sea temperatures around 16°C. Expect sunny intervals with occasional cloud, light winds (5–10 knots), and low swell. Ideal for cruising, but bring a light jacket for sunset.

faq.q: Which yachts are available for Easter Week?
faq.a: Our full fleet is available, including Astondoa 40 (9 pax, €749/2h), Azimut 39 (11 pax, €749/2h), Mangusta 80 (12 pax, €4,719/4h), and more. See our [fleet overview](/blog/) for complete options.

We do not run seasonal discounts, but Tier B charters (Mangusta 80 variants) include a free jet ski for 4-hour-plus bookings. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht for just a few hours during Easter?
faq.a: Yes. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at 2-hour charters from €749. For longer experiences, 8-hour bookings cost €2,299. Tier B boats require a minimum 4-hour booking. Contact us via WhatsApp for other boats.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad during my Easter charter?
faq.a: Your safety is paramount. If adverse weather (e.g., strong winds, storms) is forecast, we will reschedule or offer a full refund. We monitor conditions 48 hours prior and keep you informed.

faq.q: How do I check real-time availability for Easter Week?
faq.a: Availability changes daily. The best way is to WhatsApp us directly via the number on our site. We respond within hours and can confirm your preferred date and vessel instantly.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or decorations for an Easter celebration?
faq.a: Absolutely. You are welcome to bring additional food, cakes, or decorations. Our standard provision includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Let us know in advance if you need a cooler or extra storage.

data.body_html:
Easter Week (Semana Santa) in Marbella is one of the busiest periods for yacht charters. With warm spring sun, calm Mediterranean waters, and a surge of visitors, securing a boat requires early planning. This guide covers marbella easter week yacht availability, booking windows, pricing, and tips to lock in your preferred vessel from Puerto Banús.

Why Easter Week is Peak Season in Marbella

Semana Santa draws thousands to the Costa del Sol for processions, beach time, and socialising. Yacht charters become highly sought-after because the weather is pleasant (18–22°C) and sea conditions are mild. Most visitors want afternoon cruises along the coast from Marbella to Cabopino or Estepona, making afternoon slots the first to sell out.

Booking Window: When to Reserve

For Easter Week 2025, aim to book 4–6 weeks in advance. That means late February to early March. Last-minute availability is rare, especially for larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (12 pax). Smaller boats like the Dubhe (5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax) have slightly more flexibility but still fill up by mid-March.

Our Fleet & Easter Pricing

All charters depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

BoatCapacityEaster Price (2h)Easter Price (8h)
Astondoa 409 pax€749€2,299
Azimut 3911 pax€749€2,299
Mangusta 80 (any variant)12 pax€4,719 (4h min)N/A
Other boatsVariesPricing on request via WhatsApp

For Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80, a minimum 4-hour charter at €4,719 includes a free jet ski. Perfect for Easter fun on the water.

Popular Easter Routes from Puerto Banús

Most Easter charters follow a classic route: head east toward Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile, then west to Cabopino or Cala del Faro. For longer trips (6–8 hours), many guests cruise to Estepona or even Sotogrande. The sea is calm, with average swell under 0.5m in April.

What to Expect Weather-Wise

Easter Week in Marbella offers daytime highs of 18–22°C, sea temperature around 16°C, and light winds (5–10 knots). Pack a light jacket or jumper for the evening return. Sunscreen and sunglasses are essential—the spring sun is deceptively strong.

How to Secure Your Easter Charter

To check marbella easter week yacht availability, WhatsApp us directly. We respond within hours and can confirm your preferred date, boat, and duration. Do not rely on online calendars alone—real-time availability shifts quickly. For more tips, read our [Marbella boat rental guide](/blog/).

Frequently asked questions

When should I book for Easter Week in Marbella?

Book 4 to 6 weeks before Easter Week (Semana Santa) to secure your preferred yacht. March–April is a transitional period with increasing demand, so early booking avoids disappointment, especially for larger groups needing 12-pax vessels.

What is the weather like for Easter Week yacht charters?

Easter Week in Marbella typically sees daytime highs of 18–22°C and sea temperatures around 16°C. Expect sunny intervals with occasional cloud, light winds (5–10 knots), and low swell. Ideal for cruising, but bring a light jacket for sunset.

Which yachts are available for Easter Week?

Our full fleet is available, including Astondoa 40 (9 pax, €749/2h), Azimut 39 (11 pax, €749/2h), Mangusta 80 (12 pax, €4,719/4h), and more. See our [fleet overview](/blog/) for complete options.

Are there any Easter-specific promotions?

We do not run seasonal discounts, but Tier B charters (Mangusta 80 variants) include a free jet ski for 4-hour-plus bookings. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

Can I charter a yacht for just a few hours during Easter?

Yes. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at 2-hour charters from €749. For longer experiences, 8-hour bookings cost €2,299. Tier B boats require a minimum 4-hour booking. Contact us via WhatsApp for other boats.

What happens if the weather is bad during my Easter charter?

Your safety is paramount. If adverse weather (e.g., strong winds, storms) is forecast, we will reschedule or offer a full refund. We monitor conditions 48 hours prior and keep you informed.

How do I check real-time availability for Easter Week?

Availability changes daily. The best way is to WhatsApp us directly via the number on our site. We respond within hours and can confirm your preferred date and vessel instantly.

Can I bring my own food or decorations for an Easter celebration?

Absolutely. You are welcome to bring additional food, cakes, or decorations. Our standard provision includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Let us know in advance if you need a cooler or extra storage.
